question: All are signs of hydrocephalus in a neonate except –a)  Enlarged headb)  Sunset signc) Crack post signd) Depressed fontanelle

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Enlarged head (Option a) - True, as an enlarged head is a classic sign of hydrocephalus.
Sunset sign (Option b) - True, described as part of the typical presentations of hydrocephalus where pupils may look closer to the lower eyelids.
Crack post sigh (Option c) - False, crack post sigh does not relate directly to signs of hydrocephalus.
Depressed fontanelle (Option d) - True, although less commonly stated compared to others, a bulging fontanelle indicates increased intracranial pressure consistent with hydrocephalus.
Therefore, the option that does not match the signs listed specifically related to hydrocephalus is
Answer: C
